title: oxalis alpina
intro: Oxalis alpina, also known as the Alpine Wood Sorrel, is a perennial plant species belonging to the wood sorrel family (Oxalidaceae). The plant's leaves, stems, and flowers have been used in traditional medicine for their medicinal properties, which include being a natural diuretic and having antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties. Consuming Oxalis alpina is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing uric acid levels and alleviating symptoms of gout, as well as providing antioxidant properties that can help protect against cell damage. The plant's parts can be prepared as herbal infusions, teas, or tinctures, and it's also used as a flavoring agent in some recipes due to its sour taste, similar to lemon.
